title: Sunburn
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Sunburn is skin reddening after overexposure to the sun or other ultraviolet light.
Symptoms may include red painful skin, warmth, tenderness, and peeling.
Home care includes avoiding further sun exposure, cool baths or compresses, and fluids as appropriate.
Severe sunburn with blistering, fever, chills, weakness, dehydration, or eye symptoms needs clinician care.
